mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut periods, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity sell-offs over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear regimes with a t-stat > 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand that sustains corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts yield negative 60-day forward returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in the index of industrial production during a supply-constrained regime reflects bottleneck pressures that compress profit margins and force negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns in >65% of observations, or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Lower monthly inflation readings reduce expectations for aggressive central bank tightening, supporting higher equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Declining CPIAUCSL values predict negative 5-day forward returns in >55% of cases, or the autocorrelation-adjusted t-stat falls below 1.0 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP growth below 1.0% signals weakening aggregate demand that depresses corporate revenue forecasts, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth below 1.0% is followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in >60% of instances, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal yields negative 20-day forward returns in >55% of triggered months, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid federal funds rate hikes during a high-inflation regime trigger liquidity withdrawal and higher discount rates, forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rate hikes during high inflation produce positive 60-day SPX returns in >50% of cases, or the predictive slope reverses sign when CPIAUCSL falls below 3.0%.
